ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON FEB-15-2011 IN 220, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL. JENNIFER ALESIO APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFFS. DAVID J. KESTENBAUM APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT HAMILTON MATERIALS INC. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BE REFILED BASED ON A SUFFICIENT EVIDENTIARY SHOWING AFTER DEBRA KINKEL HAS BEEN DEPOSED. THE ABSENCE OF ANY DECLARATION FROM WILLIAM KINKEL, HIS MOTHER OR ANYONE ELSE WITH PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE ABOUT WILLIAM'S RELATIONSHIP WITH AND RELIANCE ON HIS GRANDFATHER RENDERS THE MOTION DEFICIENT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (220)

David J. Kestenbaum, Esq., of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / opposing party Hamilton Materials, Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS ALVIN KINKEL, DEBRA KINKEL, WILLIAM KINKEL'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is denied without prejudice. There is no evidentiary support to show that William Kinkel has a substantial interest in the case. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
